title: 15 Indians killed in Vietnam tragedy: 10 from TN, 3 from Andhra - embassy releases list
content: Fifteen Indian nationals perished in a tragic boat accident off Vietnam's coast on Saturday. The speedboat carrying thirty-six people capsized near May Rut Ngoai Islet, prompting a swift rescue effort. Tourist boats arrived quickly, but some passengers remained trapped inside the overturned vessel. The Indian embassy released the names of the deceased, with most hailing from Tamil Nadu.

title: 'Poisoned soft drink': Man kills son, dies by suicide over wife's extended stay at parents' home
content: A man allegedly poisoned his four-year-old son and himself after a dispute with his wife. The wife had refused to leave her parental home following her sister's wedding ceremony. The child died en route to the hospital after his condition deteriorated rapidly. The father later consumed poison and died during treatment at the hospital. Police are investigating the exact circumstances surrounding this tragic incident.

title: 'Stabbed 10 times': Nursing student killed after blocking stalker's calls
content: A 24-year-old man was arrested for fatally stabbing a 19-year-old pharmacy student. The victim had rejected his advances and blocked his phone number previously. He confronted her in her rented room and attacked her with a knife. The accused had also threatened her brother and her café employer. Police arrested the man after he fled towards Raipur following the incident.

title: Google CEO Sundar Pichai on the one AI race Google is ‘losing’ to Anthropic and OpenAI
content: Google's CEO acknowledges falling behind rivals in agentic coding technology.  This crucial AI capability has become the industry's most lucrative battleground.  Google's new tools, Antigravity 2.0 and Gemini 3.5 Flash, aim to close this gap.  Internal adoption shows promise, but external usage lags behind competitors.  Google is also adjusting pricing to attract heavy coding workloads.

title: Pakistan turns to army chief Asim Munir in battle against population growth
content: Asim Munir, the chief of the Pakistani army, has taken a notable role in a prominent committee tasked with tackling the pressing issue of population growth in the country. The committee aims to devise strategies to manage the rapidly increasing numbers. Federal Health Minister Syed Mustafa Kamal emphasized the critical nature of this initiative, in addition to talks on revising the National Finance Commission Award system.

title: India tops China on new millionaires, keeps money the old way
content: India added over 31,000 new dollar millionaires in 2025, significantly outpacing China.  Indian wealthy individuals hold most assets in real estate and gold, unlike Western economies.  This preference for tangible assets contrasts with a greater financialization seen elsewhere.  While wealth is growing, it remains concentrated in a few major cities.

title: Mom lying dead, son rushes for help: Indian-origin techie killed by husband in Georgia
content: In a shocking domestic violence incident in Georgia, a man is charged after allegedly shooting his son and killing his wife. The injured son sought refuge with neighbors while Sheetal Wrzesien, the victim, a tech leader, was lying dead inside the house. The husband was arrested and charged with felony murder.

title: Melinda Gates to students: When a woman steps into power, it is better for all
content: Melinda French Gates received the Josef Korbel Award for her philanthropic work. She stressed that empowering women builds stronger families and nations. French Gates highlighted the importance of centering women in philanthropic efforts. Her organization Pivotal invests in entrepreneurs and advocates for paid leave. She urged students to contribute their skills to local communities.

title: 'Jungle was brutal, but it healed me': Jharkhand girl's journey to stardom
content: Sudipta Mondol overcame childhood loneliness and parental indifference to build her own life. She navigated a difficult marriage and emerged stronger after a challenging divorce. Mondol then rebuilt her confidence by pursuing fitness and learning new skills. Her participation in Naked and Afraid, aadimanav showcased her incredible resilience and adaptability. This journey highlights transformation through overcoming significant personal obstacles.

title: Elon Musk calls Sam Altman scammer after Apple accuses OpenAI of stealing trade secrets
content: Elon Musk has publicly criticized OpenAI CEO Sam Altman on social media. This follows Apple's lawsuit accusing OpenAI of corporate espionage and stealing trade secrets. OpenAI has denied these allegations, stating their focus is on innovation. Musk previously warned about Altman's alleged deceptive practices. The legal dispute highlights tensions between major tech entities.

title: Legendary singer S Janaki passes away at 88 in Mysuru due to age-related ailments
content: Veteran playback singer S Janaki, known as Nightingale of South India, passed away at 88. She died at Apollo Hospital in Mysuru after battling age-related health issues. Janaki Amma had declined the Padma Bhushan award in 2013, feeling it came too late. She received four National Film Awards and thirty-three State Film Awards during her career. The legendary singer had lent her voice to over twenty Indian languages.

title: As India remain stuck in traffic, England players enjoy football - Watch
content: Ahead of the fifth T20I in Southampton, England players played a game of rondo football after India's arrival was delayed due to traffic. The delay pushed the toss to 7:15 PM IST and the start to 7:30 PM IST. Once India reached the venue, Shreyas Iyer and Suryansh Shedge began warm-ups as England chased a 4-0 series win.

title: 7/12, Khata, Jamabandi or Patta? A guide to land ownership documents across India
content: Land records are crucial for various property transactions across India. Different states use unique names like 7/12 extract and Khatauni for these documents. These records detail ownership, cultivation, and tax liabilities for land parcels. While terminology differs, their core purpose remains consistent for all citizens. Digitization efforts are making this vital information increasingly accessible online.

title: Who are Lamine Yamal's parents? Meet Mounir Nasraoui and Sheila Ebana
content: Lamine Yamal's parents, Mounir Nasraoui from Morocco and Sheila Ebana from Equatorial Guinea, both immigrated to Spain and raised Yamal in the greater Barcelona area under modest financial conditions. After separating when Yamal was around three years old, his mother raised him in Rocafonda, Mataró, while working to make ends meet. His name honors two men who helped his family during a period of hardship. Despite his Spanish nationality and international football career with La Roja, Yamal proudly represents his Moroccan and Equatorial Guinean heritage, wearing his parents' national flags on his boots at every match.

title: NHL trade rumors: Canucks eye Maple Leafs' $46.5m winger
content: Matthew Knies trade speculation continues to build around the Toronto Maple Leafs, with the Vancouver Canucks emerging as a team being actively encouraged to pursue the 23-year-old winger. Knies, coming off a 66-point season and carrying a $7. 75M cap hit through 2031, holds no trade protection in his contract. While Leafs management views him as core to their future, rival teams keep calling. Any realistic offer would need to include NHL-ready defensive pieces, premium draft picks, and a top prospect to get Toronto's attention.

title: Matthew Tkachuk's emotional farewell hints at Maple Leafs' major NHL addition
content: Matthew Tkachuk has offered an emotional tribute to Sergei Bobrovsky after the veteran goalie left the Florida Panthers for the Toronto Maple Leafs. Tkachuk lauded Bobrovsky for his leadership, consistency and ability to win during the Panthers’ Stanley Cup run. The move gives Toronto a proven NHL winner as the franchise continues to look for more playoff stability and success in the postseason.

title: 'Operation Lotus' in J&K? CM Omar Abdullah claims BJP offered MLAs Rs 20-30 crore
content: Jammu and Kashmir's Chief Minister Omar Abdullah condemned the Bharatiya Janata Party for what he called attempts to destabilize his administration. He revealed that BJP legislators were allegedly offered lucrative financial rewards and government positions to switch loyalties. Abdullah claimed the BJP's promises of statehood to National Conference MLAs were a tactic to sway them.

title: Jayden Adams cause of death: South Africa mourns World Cup midfielder
content: South Africa midfielder Jayden Adams has died at 25, just weeks after featuring at the FIFA World Cup 2026. His cause of death has not been disclosed. The Mamelodi Sundowns star earned widespread tributes from football officials, teammates, and fans, who remembered his talent, commitment, and bright future after representing Bafana Bafana on the world stage.

title: Kerala toddler dies after anaesthesia for lip cut; doctor booked for negligence
content: An anaesthetist faces negligence charges after a toddler died following anaesthesia administration. The one-and-a-half-year-old boy received anaesthesia for a lip injury treatment. His condition worsened, and he was moved to another hospital for further care. The child unfortunately passed away while undergoing intensive treatment in the ICU. Hospital authorities have denied the allegations and stated all possible treatment was provided.

title: Rs 17L looted in 28 seconds: CCTV captures gunpoint robbery by bike-borne men in Delhi
content: An employee was robbed of seventeen lakh rupees in east Delhi's Gandhi Nagar market. Two bike-borne assailants threatened him at gunpoint and snatched his cash bag. CCTV footage shows the incident occurring around 4:09 pm on Friday afternoon. Police are analyzing the video to identify and apprehend the two accused individuals. The preliminary investigation suggests the robbers had been following the victim.

title: Raja blasts PCB over Masood sacking: 'When a captain is given ... '
content: Former Pakistan captain Ramiz Raja criticised the PCB for removing Shan Masood as Test captain and reinstating Babar Azam. Defending Shan, Raja said the skipper was unfairly blamed despite having an "ordinary" squad, adding, “If the standard of the Test squad is not good, how is the captain only responsible for this.” Pakistan next tour the West Indies and England.

title: Pawan Kalyan undergoes 3.5-hour shoulder surgery in Mumbai after injury
content: Andhra Pradesh Deputy Chief Minister Pawan Kalyan had successful shoulder surgery in Mumbai. Doctors performed a three-and-a-half-hour procedure on his right shoulder. This surgery addressed severe rotator cuff injuries and an avulsion fracture. His injuries originally sustained in 2016 worsened over years of public interactions. Surgery for his left shoulder is planned in approximately two months.

title: India stuck in traffic: Why was the toss delayed for IND vs ENG 5th T20I?
content: The toss for the fifth T20I between India and England at Southampton was delayed after the Indian team got stuck in traffic while travelling to the Rose Bowl. Live visuals later showed the team arriving at the venue. The toss was rescheduled for 7:15 PM IST, with the match set to begin at 7:30 PM IST, barring further delays.

title: Erling Haaland's girlfriend opens up about protecting family from spotlight
content: Erling Haaland's World Cup success has renewed interest in his private life with longtime girlfriend Isabel Haugseng Johansen. The former footballer, now a fashion creator and mother, says she wants to protect her family's privacy while allowing people to know her better. The couple, who began dating after Johansen messaged Haaland on Instagram, continue to keep their son out of the public eye.

title: Pakistan claims 88 militants killed in ongoing Balochistan crackdown
content: Pakistan's security forces have killed eighty-eight militants in Balochistan since July fifth.  Operation Shaban involves the army and paramilitary forces targeting militant hideouts.  This operation began after coordinated attacks rocked the province on July fifth.  Militants killed nine police personnel and abducted eighteen others at a checkpoint.  Violence also struck a local tribe, killing five and abducting eleven individuals.

title: From Khan Market to CP: What's driving rents across Delhi-NCR's premium retail hubs
content: Delhi's prime retail rents saw significant year-on-year increases during the April-June quarter. Khan Market experienced a nine percent rise, retaining its top position as India's most expensive. South Extension markets recorded the sharpest ten percent rental growth, while other areas showed varied trends. Retail space leasing volumes more than doubled, with malls dominating quarterly take-up. Annual leasing activity reached a six-year high, driven by main street locations.

title: 'Providing all possible assistance': PM Modi after Vietnam boat accident claims 15 Indian lives
content: Fifteen Indian tourists died in a tragic speedboat accident near Vietnam's Phu Quoc Island. Several other tourists sustained injuries during the unfortunate maritime incident. Prime Minister Narendra Modi expressed deep sorrow and offered condolences to affected families. The government is providing full support and coordinating with Vietnamese authorities. Search and rescue operations were conducted by local authorities after the boat capsized.

title: Watch: 'Viking Row' fever grips Miami before Norway's historic World Cup clash against England
content: Norway fans transform Miami Beach into a vibrant celebration before their World Cup quarter-final match.  The "Viking Row" tradition has become a viral sensation, drawing local and tourist participation.  Star striker Erling Haaland leads Norway's potent attack, which also features other talented players.  England enters the game with confidence after a dramatic victory in their previous match.  Both teams will contend with Miami's heat and humidity in this historic fixture.

title: 'Heavy winds and high waves': Vietnam boat tragedy kills 15 Indians - what we know so far
content: Tragedy struck as a speedboat transporting fifteen Indian tourists capsized near Phu Quoc island in Vietnam, claiming their lives. The accident occurred while the vessel was making its return from Hon May Rut Island to An Thoi Port, where thirty-six passengers were onboard. Rescue operations faced challenges due to rough sea conditions and individuals trapped within the vessel.

title: Harry Kane vs Erling Haaland: Who is better striker?
content: Ahead of England's 2026 FIFA World Cup quarter-final against Norway, Harry Kane and Erling Haaland remain closely matched in the debate over football's best striker. Kane leads in overall goals, experience and his remarkable 2025-26 season, while Haaland boasts a record-breaking Premier League scoring rate and superior international efficiency. Their contrasting styles, achievements and records make their World Cup showdown one of the tournament's biggest attractions.

title: Aamir Khan's parenting advice every Indian parent should hear
content: Aamir Khan prioritizes his children's happiness and curiosity over academic success. He aims to raise children who value simplicity and independence from his wealth. Khan advises parents to offer guidance only when their children specifically ask for it. He encourages chasing excellence rather than mere success in life. The actor stresses that caring is the most important quality a child can possess.

title: 2013 terror attack: SIA secures interpol red corner notice against Hizbul terrorist
content: Jammu and Kashmir Police's SIA secured an Interpol Red Corner Notice for Hizbul Mujahideen terrorist Imtiyaz Ahmad Kandoo. This notice is a significant development in the 2013 terror case investigation. The attack in Baramulla district tragically claimed the lives of four police personnel. Kandoo, an active commander since 2010, was designated a terrorist in October 2022. The notice aims to aid in apprehending the designated individual for his alleged involvement.

title: Slithering snakes, lakhs on the run: Twin typhoons batter China; scary visuals emerge
content: Chinese authorities evacuated over 900,000 people ahead of Typhoon Bavi's expected landfall. The storm is forecast to hit Wenzhou after affecting Taiwan and Japan's islands. Taiwan evacuated 14,000 people and faced widespread power outages and flight cancellations. Japan's southwestern islands also experienced significant power disruptions and flight cancellations. The Philippines reported 18 deaths due to heavy rains intensified by the typhoon.

title: US lawmaker says Israeli settlers armed with US-made rifles detained him in West Bank
content: US Representative Ro Khanna reported being detained by armed Israeli settlers.  The incident occurred while he was visiting a destroyed Palestinian village.  Israeli security forces eventually intervened and dispersed the settlers.  Khanna stated the trip strengthened his resolve for a presidential run.  He also criticized US support for Israel and called for action against alleged genocide.

title: Shraddha Walkar murder accused to write MA exam in jail; hearing deferred
content: A Delhi court postponed the Shraddha Walkar murder case hearing. Accused Aaftab Poonawala is appearing for his final MA Sociology examination. His exams are scheduled inside Tihar Jail from July 11 to July 20. The court accepted his plea and cancelled the July 20 proceedings. Prosecution evidence recording will now begin from July 21.

title: 1 in 4 adults with diabetes may have hearing loss: Tips to protect your ears
content: Adults with type two diabetes face increased hearing loss risks. Persistently high blood sugar can damage inner ear blood vessels and cells. Research indicates diabetes patients have double the odds of significant hearing impairment. Early detection and good blood sugar control are strongly advised by experts. Awareness of this lesser-known complication is becoming increasingly important globally.

title: Kushare scripts history on Diamond League debut, becomes first Indian ever to...
content: India's Sarvesh Kushare finished third in the men's high jump on his Diamond League debut at the Monaco meet, clearing 2. 26m. He became the first Indian to secure a top-three finish on a Diamond League debut and only the fourth Indian to achieve the feat overall. The podium finish continues his impressive season ahead of the 2026 Commonwealth Games.

title: Indian tourist drowns while swimming in Nepal's Phewa Lake
content: A 28-year-old Indian tourist from Bihar drowned in Nepal's Phewa Lake. Rohit Kumar, who was visiting Pokhara with friends, died after swimming. A disaster management team launched a search operation for the missing tourist. He was rescued and rushed to a hospital for treatment. Authorities are currently investigating the unfortunate drowning incident.

title: 'Come to rear seat': Conductor held for exposing his private parts to students
content: A 32-year-old bus conductor was arrested for alleged sexual harassment.  Irfan exposed himself and made obscene gestures inside a private bus.  He targeted college students and asked them to move to the rear.  Police registered a case based on a complaint filed by students.  Further investigation into the incident is currently ongoing.

title: Ghaziabad horror: 7-year-old raped and murdered, body found in mall basement
content: A seven-year-old girl was allegedly kidnapped and murdered in Ghaziabad. Her body was discovered in the basement of an under-construction mall. Family members claim she was lured with snacks before being assaulted. Police have detained several individuals for questioning regarding the incident. An investigation is underway, and an autopsy report is awaited.

title: DVAC arrests seven govt. staff in one week; seizes over ₹22 lakh in surprise checks
content: Seven government employees were arrested across the State in inspection conducted between July 4 and 10 for allegedly demanding and accepting bribes for performing official duties

title: Search for lone missing worker in Wayanad debris slip continues
content: Joint task force’s efforts to trace Bikram Singh Rana, a project manager from Himachal Pradesh, yields no results as of late July 11 evening

title: Operation Toofan will continue until drug networks are dismantled: Chennithala
content: Home Minister launches year-long anti-drug campaign and student-led awareness initiative at Chalakudy school

title: Ram temple donation 'theft' a disgrace; we feel humiliated: Nripendra Mishra
content: ‘This is [now] an issue about temple management, and the management will surely be improved to guard against a repeat of this [alleged donation theft],’ the Ram Temple construction committee’s chairperson says; a high-level committee had been set up to suggest a suitable name as CEO

title: Thermocol dumping in Parappukkara sparks eco concerns
content: Residents allege eco-tourism project became a dumping ground for waste; panchayat to take action against unauthorised activity

title: Primary teachers’ associations to file curative petition in apex court seeking TET exemption for in-service teachers
content: They have also resolved to continue nationwide agitation until the Old Pension Scheme is restored for government employees across the country

title: CPI, Congress lock horns over Aranmula airport project
content: The controversy was triggered after Abraham Kalamannil, who owns land at the proposed project site, conducted a drone survey of the area on Friday with a team of experts brought in from Delhi

title: ‘Haram for one, halal for another’: Jaleel questions IUML’s divided stance on lighting the lamp
content: Function attended by Ministers P.K. Kunhalikutty and K.M. Shaji in Thiruvananthapuram on July 9 revives party’s long-running debate over the practice as while Shaji lit the nilavilakku, Kunhalikutty refused to do so

title: Hyderabad couple disappear after claiming to fly to Switzerland; police investigate potential ₹50-crore chit fund scam
content: Police probe whether the businessman and his wife ever left India; no fraud complaint has been registered so far.

title: Kharge calls Madhya Pradesh ‘epicentre of corruption’, cites alleged ₹1,200-crore rice scamt
content: Congress alleges nearly five lakh metric tonnes of rice had been siphoned off in a “game of profit” and claims diversion took place with the connivance of government machinery.

title: National Lok Adalat resolves 38,412 cases in one day in Nellore district
content: Participating in the programme, District Principal Judge says resolving disputes through compromise not only saves time and money but also ensures speedy justice

title: BJP targets Congress over long-term tenders, Bidadi township project
content: R. Ashok accuses Congress government of pushing long-term projects for vested interests; B.Y. Vijayendra says BJP, not Congress, has worked for AHINDA communities

title: A paraglider’s journey from the hills of Vagamon to the European skies
content: Hailing from high ranges of Kottayam, Jobin Sebastian is set to represent UK at the European Paragliding Accuracy Championship

title: Al to assist and not to decide equities: Chief Justice of India Surya Kant
content: Whether digital dispute resolution and AI enhance or disrupt access to justice depends on how thoughtfully and deliberately their use is designed, says Chief Justice Kant

title: Become entrepreneurs and generate jobs, says A.P. Governor to JNTU-GV graduates
content: JNTU-GV holds its first convocation for 2019-23, 2020-24, and 2021-25 batches, conferres 34,896 degrees, including 11 Ph.D. degrees

title: A.P. CM Chandrababu Naidu expresses anguish over death of two persons from State in Vietnam boat accident
content: Of the 33 persons from Andhra Pradesh who have gone on a trip, 19 are on the boat when the accident occurred, he says, and directs officials to take steps to bring back those who survived and contact the family members of the deceased and extend them necessary support

title: On Camera, Thar Pulls Out ATM With Rope In Daring Odisha Heist
content: Locals say there have been several incidents of theft and robbery in the region over the past few months, and residents have demanded increased police patrolling and stronger security measures.

title: 15 Indian Tourists Killed As Boat Overturns In Vietnam
content: Search and rescue operations are underway, and the Indian mission has set up control rooms to assist those affected.

title: Funding Crunch Threatens Future of Women's Support NGOs Worldwide
content: At least one million women and girls have lost access to critical humanitarian support since January 2025 as aid funding cuts has forced women-led organisations to scale back services across some of the world's most vulnerable regions.

title: Assets Worth Rs 1,021 Crore In Reliance Anil Ambani Group Case Attached By Probe Agency
content: The Enforcement Directorate (ED) on Saturday said it has attached fresh properties worth Rs 1,021 crore in the Reliance Home Finance Limited (RHFL) and Reliance Commercial Finance Limited (RCFL) case.

title: Vietnam Boat Tragedy Updates: PM Modi Condoles Deaths Of Indian Tourists In Vietnam
content: Indian Tourists Killed in Vietnam Boat Incident News Updates: At least 15 Indians, including 13 men and 2 women, were killed in the incident. There were a total of 36 people on board the vessel.

title: Omar Abdullah Says MLA Was Offered Rs 20 Crore To Defect, BJP Slams Claim
content: Abdullah claimed that the offer was made by a Supreme Court lawyer associated with the BJP during a closed-door meeting with the MLA. He said the legislator personally informed him of the alleged offer.

title: Hizbul Commander Who Ambushed Cops In 2013 Faces Interpol Heat Now
content: The State Investigation Agency (SIA) Kashmir announced today that Interpol has issued a Red Corner Notice against Imtiyaz Ahmad Kandoo, a Hizbul Mujahideen terrorist

title: 36 Aboard Speedboat, Rough Seas: What We Know About Vietnam Tragedy
content: At least 15 Indian tourists were killed after a speedboat overturned near Vietnam's Phu Quoc Island today. It was carrying 32 tourists and four crew from Hon May Rut to An Thoi Port when it sank. The Indian embassy has shared more information.

title: "Pledge To Avenge His Blood": Mojtaba Khamenei On Father's Funeral
content: The statement was released on the occasion of the funeral ceremonies of Ali Khamenei, which were held months after he was killed in the US-Israeli strikes on his office in Tehran on February 28.

title: Explained: BJP's High-Stakes Gamble In Punjab
content: BJP sources said the high command has already deployed party leaders to consolidate particular segments of voters in Punjab

title: Satluj Movie Screened In Gurdwara In Jammu
content: In large numbers, people converged at Gurdwara Saheb in Nanak Nagar on Friday to watch the film on a big screen - free of cost.

title: 4 Hours On Roof With Woman's Body: Flood-Hit Family's Wait For Rescue
content: 55-year-old Anusya Manohar Leelaka had passed away. The family was still mourning her loss when floodwaters rapidly began to enter their home.

title: Bengal Forms High-Level Committee To Review Draft Uniform Civil Code Bill
content: A government notification issued on Friday said the committee was formed in view of the "wide ramifications and voluminous nature" of the proposed legislation.

title: Mountains Of Cash, Weapons Found At Don Baba Farzan's Home In Maharashtra
content: In the 1990s, Don Baba Farzan established a strong influence and terror in Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar and its surrounding areas.

title: 'Goes Against India's Sovereignty': Government Panel Backs Ban On 'Satluj'
content: The Honey Trehan-directed film depicts the life of human rights activist Jaswant Singh Khalra, who investigated the cremation of thousands of unidentified bodies in Punjab between 1984 and 1994

title: Falling Debris Puts 400 Families In Greater Noida's Janata Flats At Risk
content: Plaster has been peeling off walls, while chhajjas (balconies) have been frequently collapsing, turning the housing complex into a potential hazard.

title: China's New Mega Dam Near Indian Border Poses Huge Geological Risk: Report
content: China is building the world's largest hydroelectric dam in the lower parts of the Yarlung Tsangpo River in Tibet, about 50 kilometres from the border with India.

title: Congress demands Supreme Court-monitored probe into Ram temple donation row
content: AICC general secretary accuses BJP of betraying the faith of millions of devotees by ‘allowing financial irregularities’ in the management of donations collected for Ram temple in Ayodhya

title: Ananda Group chairman to receive lifetime achievement award
content: Awards committee chairman says three others will also be receive the award at a function which will be held in New Delhi on July 28

title: At least 15 Indian tourists dead after boat capsizes near island off Vietnam; rescue efforts under way
content: For any assistance, the first control room can be reached at: +84 36 281 7930, +84 91 552 37 14 and +84 33 452 0414; the other one in Hanoi can be reached at: +84 91 308 9165

title: New Zealand PM Luxon hails India’s ‘incredible’ growth story, urges stronger business ties
content: Stressing that there are many areas for India and New Zealand to collaborate, Mr. Luxon named food and produce, tourism experiences, educational institutions, and technology as sectors for possible collaboration

title: Karnataka High Court refuses to quash FIR against school heads for allegedly suppressing sexual assault complaint by minor against roommate
content: A complaint by the victim’s father alleged that the child welfare officer destroyed his son’s original written complaint and compelled him to submit a fresh statement portraying the incident as a ‘consensual act’.

title: Assembly elections likely in mid-2029 after delimitation, Congress to retain power: Telangana Chief Minister A. Revanth Reddy
content: He forecast that the Congress would win 117 Assembly seats in the next elections and form the government for a second successive term

title: Minister distributes CMRF cheques worth ₹59.42 lakhs to medical beneficiaries
content: After distributing the cheques at his Banaganapalle camp office, Janardhan Reddy inquires about the current health status and well-being of the patients who received the medical aid

title: A.P. Chief Minister Chandrababu Naidu launches ‘Pillale Sampada’ scheme to achieve demographic stability
content: Under the scheme, a financial assistance of ₹30,000 will be given for the birth of a third child and ₹40,000 for the fourth one

title: West Bengal conducts filariasis drug drive in 10 districts, targets elimination by 2030
content: Health workers administer free preventive medicines in 59 blocks and urban local bodies; officials urge eligible residents to consume the drugs under direct observation

title: Where retirement goes to die: Inside America’s geriatric government
content: average age of lawmakers among highest in modern american history . 84-year-old mitch McConnell was hospitalized after prolonged absence from the Senate . his current status has prompted another round of soul-searching over a peculiar feature of American democracy .
